mn37:0.2: The Shorter Discourse on the Ending of Craving
mn37:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in the Eastern Park, at Migāra’s Mother’s Mansion.
mn37:10.1: Just as a daughter-in-law, on seeing her father-in-law, becomes abashed and embarrassed,
mn37:10.2: so too, when Sakka’s attendant women saw Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, they became abashed and embarrassed, and each entered her own chamber.
mn37:10.3: Then Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, and Vessavaṇa the Great King led Venerable Mahāmoggallāna walking around the Vejayanta mansion and showing it to him:
mn37:10.4: “Look at this too, sir Moggallāna, the beauty of the Vejayanta mansion;
mn37:10.5: look at this too, sir Moggallāna, the beauty of the Vejayanta mansion.”
mn37:10.6: “This is splendid for you, Kosiya, as for one who made merit in the past.
mn37:10.7: Even human beings, on seeing something beautiful, say:
mn37:10.8: ‘Indeed, it is splendid, sirs, like that of the Tāvatiṁsa *devatās*.’
mn37:10.9: And this is splendid for you, Kosiya, as for one who made merit in the past.”
mn37:11.1: Then it occurred to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna:
mn37:11.2: “This spirit is living far too heedlessly.
mn37:11.3: What if I were to shake this spirit up?”
mn37:11.4: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna performed such an exertion of psychic power that with his big toe he made the Vejayanta mansion shake, quake, and tremble.
mn37:11.5: Then Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, Vessavaṇa the Great King, and the Tāvatiṁsa *devatās* became filled with amazement and wonder:
mn37:11.6: “It is wonderful, sirs! It is amazing, sirs!
mn37:11.7: Such is the ascetic’s great psychic power and great might, that he can make a celestial residence shake, quake, and tremble with his big toe!”
mn37:12.1: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, knowing that Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, was alarmed and had goosebumps, said this to him:
mn37:12.2: “How then, Kosiya, did the *Bhagavā* explain in brief liberation by the ending of craving?
mn37:12.3: *Sādhu*, may we too share in hearing this discussion.”
mn37:12.4: “Here, sir Moggallāna, I approached the *Bhagavā*. Having approached, I paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and stood to one side. Standing to one side, sir Moggallāna, I said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn37:12.5: ‘To what extent, bhante, is a *bhikkhu*, in brief, liberated by the ending of craving, one who has reached the ultimate goal, ultimate security from bondage, ultimate holy life, ultimate consummation, foremost among *devatās* and humans?’
mn37:13.1: When this was said, sir Moggallāna, the *Bhagavā* said this to me:
mn37:13.10: They understand: “Birth is ended, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.”
mn37:13.11: To this extent, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu*, in brief, is liberated by the ending of craving, one who has reached the ultimate goal, ultimate security from bondage, ultimate holy life, ultimate consummation, foremost among *devatās* and humans.’
mn37:13.12: That is how, sir Moggallāna, the *Bhagavā* explained in brief liberation by the ending of craving to me.”
mn37:13.2: ‘Here, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu* has heard:
mn37:13.3: “All *dhammas* are not fit to be held onto.”
mn37:13.4: And when, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu* has heard this:
mn37:13.5: “All *dhammas* are not fit to be held onto,”
mn37:13.6: they directly know every *dhamma*; having directly known every *dhamma*, they fully understand every *dhamma*; having fully understood every *dhamma*, whatever *vedanā* they feel—whether *sukha*, *dukkha*, or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*.
mn37:13.7: They dwell contemplating those *vedanās* as impermanent [*aniccānupassī*], contemplating fading away, contemplating cessation, contemplating relinquishment.
mn37:13.8: Dwelling contemplating those *vedanās* as impermanent [*aniccānupassī*], contemplating fading away, contemplating cessation, contemplating relinquishment, they do not cling or feed on [*upādiyati*] anything in the world,
mn37:13.9: without clinging or feeding [*anupādiyaṁ*], they do not tremble. Not trembling, they personally attain final *nibbāna*.
mn37:14.1: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, having delighted in and approved of what Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, said—just as a strong man might extend a bent arm or bend an extended arm—vanished from among the Tāvatiṁsa *devatās* and appeared in Migāra’s Mother’s Mansion in the Eastern Park.
mn37:14.2: Then, not long after Venerable Mahāmoggallāna had departed, Sakka’s attendant women said this to Sakka, lord of the *devatās*:
mn37:14.4: “No, sir, that *Bhagavā* is not my teacher.
mn37:14.5: Venerable Mahāmoggallāna is my companion in the holy life.”
mn37:14.6: “It is a gain for you, sir, a great gain for you, sir,
mn37:14.7: that you have such a companion in the holy life, with such great psychic power and such great might.
mn37:14.8: How much more then must that *Bhagavā* be your teacher!”
mn37:15.1: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna approached the *Bhagavā*. Having approached, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn37:15.10: they directly know every *dhamma*; having directly known every *dhamma*, they fully understand every *dhamma*; having fully understood every *dhamma*, whatever *vedanā* they feel—whether *sukha*, *dukkha*, or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*.
mn37:15.11: They dwell contemplating those *vedanās* as impermanent [*aniccānupassī*], contemplating fading away, contemplating cessation, contemplating relinquishment.
mn37:15.12: Dwelling contemplating those *vedanās* as impermanent [*aniccānupassī*], contemplating fading away, contemplating cessation, contemplating relinquishment,
mn37:15.13: they do not cling or feed on [*upādiyati*] anything in the world,
mn37:15.14: without clinging or feeding [*anupādiyaṁ*], they do not tremble. Not trembling, they personally attain final *nibbāna*.
mn37:15.15: They understand: “Birth is ended, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.”
mn37:15.16: To this extent, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu*, in brief, is liberated by the ending of craving, one who has reached the ultimate goal, ultimate security from bondage, ultimate holy life, ultimate consummation, foremost among *devatās* and humans.’
mn37:15.17: That is how, Moggallāna, I recall having explained in brief liberation by the ending of craving to Sakka, lord of the *devatās*.”
mn37:15.19: Venerable Mahāmoggallāna was pleased and delighted in what the *Bhagavā* said.
mn37:15.2: “Does the *Bhagavā* recall, bhante, having explained in brief liberation by the ending of craving to a certain well-known and powerful spirit?”
mn37:15.20: The Shorter Discourse on the Ending of Craving is finished, the seventh.
mn37:15.3: “I do recall, Moggallāna. Here Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, approached me. Having approached, he paid homage to me and stood to one side. Standing to one side, Moggallāna, Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, said this to me:
mn37:15.4: ‘To what extent, bhante, is a *bhikkhu*, in brief, liberated by the ending of craving, one who has reached the ultimate goal, ultimate security from bondage, ultimate holy life, ultimate consummation, foremost among *devatās* and humans?’
mn37:15.5: When this was said, Moggallāna, I said this to Sakka, lord of the *devatās*:
mn37:15.6: ‘Here, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu* has heard:
mn37:15.7: “All *dhammas* are not fit to be held onto.”
mn37:15.8: And when, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu* has heard this:
mn37:15.9: “All *dhammas* are not fit to be held onto,”
mn37:2.1: Then Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, approached the *Bhagavā*. Having approached, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and stood to one side. Standing to one side, Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn37:2.2: “To what extent, bhante, is a *bhikkhu*, in brief, liberated by the ending of craving, one who has reached the ultimate goal, ultimate security from bondage, ultimate holy life, ultimate consummation, foremost among *devatās* and humans?”
mn37:3.1: “Here, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu* has heard:
mn37:3.10: To this extent, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu*, in brief, is liberated by the ending of craving, one who has reached the ultimate goal, ultimate security from bondage, ultimate holy life, ultimate consummation, foremost among *devatās* and humans.”
mn37:3.2: ‘All *dhammas* are not fit to be held onto.’
mn37:3.3: And when, lord of the *devatās*, a *bhikkhu* has heard this:
mn37:3.4: ‘All *dhammas* are not fit to be held onto,’
mn37:3.5: they directly know every *dhamma*; having directly known every *dhamma*, they fully understand every *dhamma*; having fully understood every *dhamma*, whatever *vedanā* they feel—
mn37:3.6: whether *sukha*, *dukkha*, or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*—they dwell contemplating those *vedanās* as impermanent [*aniccānupassī*], contemplating fading away, contemplating cessation, contemplating relinquishment.
mn37:3.7: Dwelling contemplating those *vedanās* as impermanent [*aniccānupassī*], contemplating fading away, contemplating cessation, contemplating relinquishment, they do not cling or feed on [*upādiyati*] anything in the world.
mn37:3.8: Without clinging or feeding [*anupādiyaṁ*], they do not tremble. Not trembling, they personally attain final *nibbāna*.
mn37:3.9: They understand: ‘Birth is ended, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.’
mn37:4.1: Then Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, having delighted in and approved of what the *Bhagavā* said, paid homage to the *Bhagavā*, went around him keeping him to his right, and vanished right there.
mn37:5.1: Now at that time Venerable Mahāmoggallāna was sitting not far from the *Bhagavā*.
mn37:5.2: Then it occurred to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna:
mn37:5.3: “Did that spirit approve of what the *Bhagavā* said after fully understanding it, or not?
mn37:5.4: What if I were to find out about that spirit—
mn37:5.5: whether that spirit approved of what the *Bhagavā* said after fully understanding it, or not?”
mn37:6.1: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna—just as a strong man might extend a bent arm or bend an extended arm—vanished from Migāra’s Mother’s Mansion in the Eastern Park and appeared among the Tāvatiṁsa *devatās*.
mn37:7.1: At that time Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, was enjoying himself in the park of the White Lotus, accompanied by five hundred celestial orchestras.
mn37:7.2: Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, saw Venerable Mahāmoggallāna coming from afar.
mn37:7.3: Seeing him, he dismissed those five hundred celestial orchestras, approached Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, and said to him:
mn37:7.5: It has been a long time, sir Moggallāna, since you found occasion to come here.
mn37:7.6: Sit down, sir Moggallāna; this seat is prepared.”
mn37:7.7: Venerable Mahāmoggallāna sat down on the prepared seat.
mn37:7.8: Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, also took a certain low seat and sat down to one side.
mn37:7.9: Sitting to one side, Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna said this to him:
mn37:8.1: “How then, Kosiya, did the *Bhagavā* explain in brief liberation by the ending of craving?
mn37:8.10: In each section there are seven hundred gabled halls.
mn37:8.11: In each gabled hall there are seven nymphs.
mn37:8.12: And each nymph has seven attendants.
mn37:8.13: Would you like, sir Moggallāna, to see the beauty of the Vejayanta mansion?”
mn37:8.2: *Sādhu*, may we too share in hearing this discussion.”
mn37:8.3: “Sir Moggallāna, I am busy and have much to do—
mn37:8.4: perhaps with my own business, and also with the business of the Tāvatiṁsa *devatās*.
mn37:8.5: Besides, sir Moggallāna, even what is well heard, well grasped, well attended to, and well retained by us quickly slips away.
mn37:8.6: Once in the past, sir Moggallāna, a battle between the *devatās* and the asuras was drawn up.
mn37:8.7: And in that battle, sir Moggallāna, the *devatās* won and the asuras were defeated.
mn37:8.8: Then I, sir Moggallāna, having won that battle and emerged victorious, on returning from there had a mansion built named Vejayanta.
mn37:8.9: Sir Moggallāna, the Vejayanta mansion has one hundred sections.
mn37:9.1: Then Sakka, lord of the *devatās*, and Vessavaṇa the Great King, placing Venerable Mahāmoggallāna in front, approached the Vejayanta mansion.
mn37:9.2: Sakka’s attendant women saw Venerable Mahāmoggallāna coming from afar.
mn37:9.3: Seeing him, feeling abashed and embarrassed, they each entered their own chamber.
